featuretablefilter 0.99.0
  • Get started
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • featuretablefilter Tutorial: Complete Guide to Microbiome Feature Table Filtering
  • Changelog

Convert feature table data.frame to phyloseq object

Source: R/convert_data_classes.R
to_phyloseq.Rd

Creates a phyloseq object from a standard feature table data.frame. Optionally incorporates taxonomy, phylogenetic tree, and sample data if provided.

to_phyloseq(
  table,
  tax_table = NULL,
  phy_tree = NULL,
  sample_data = NULL,
  feature_col = NULL
)

Arguments

table

A data.frame with feature IDs in the first column and samples as columns.

tax_table

Optional. Taxonomy table (data.frame or matrix) with feature IDs as rows and taxonomic ranks as columns (Kingdom, Phylum, Class, Order, Family, Genus, Species).

phy_tree

Optional. A phylogenetic tree (phylo object) with tip labels matching feature IDs.

sample_data

Optional. Sample metadata (data.frame) with row names matching sample names.

feature_col

Name or index of the column containing feature IDs. Default is "feature_id" or 1.

Value

A phyloseq object containing OTU table and optionally taxonomy, tree, and sample data.

Examples

if (requireNamespace("phyloseq", quietly = TRUE)) {
  data(example_feature_table)
  ps <- to_phyloseq(example_feature_table)
  print(ps)
}
#> OTU Table:          [50 taxa and 20 samples]
#>                      taxa are rows
#>         Sample_001 Sample_002 Sample_003 Sample_004 Sample_005 Sample_006
#> ASV_001       1245        892       1567        734       1123       1456
#> ASV_002        567        423        689        312        534        712
#> ASV_003        234        178        289        123        201        267
#> ASV_004         89         67        112         45         78         98
#> ASV_005       3456       2789       4123       2156       3234       3987
#> ASV_006      12345      10234      14567       8765      11234      13456
#> ASV_007       5678       4567       6789       3890       5123       6234
#> ASV_008       2345       1890       2789       1567       2123       2567
#> ASV_009        890        712       1023        567        789        934
#> ASV_010        456        367        534        278        398        478
#> ASV_011        123         98        145         76        108        132
#> ASV_012         45         34         52         27         39         48
#> ASV_013          5          3          7          2          4          6
#> ASV_014          0          1          0          0          0          1
#> ASV_015      23456      19234      26789      16789      21234      24567

#> ASV_017       3456       2789       4012       2456       3123       3678
